title: Fix open redirect with naive path check
prompt:
The following code attempts to guard against open redirects but only checks if the URL starts with `/`:

```elixir
def post_login(conn, %{"return_to" => url}) do
  if String.starts_with?(url, "/") do
    redirect(conn, external: url)
  else
    redirect(conn, to: "/")
  end
end
```

This is defeated by `//evil.com` (protocol-relative URL) and by `/\nevil.com` (CVE-2017-1000163 — the exact bypass that hit Phoenix). Rewrite the function so the redirect target is constrained to a static allowlist of safe paths, rejects any URL containing `//` at the start or newline characters, and NEVER uses `redirect(external: ...)` with user input. Use `redirect(conn, to: "/dashboard")` for the safe case. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/session_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires knowing the CVE-2017-1000163 bypass and choosing allowlist over blocklist.
research_citation: CVE-2017-1000163: 'An example of a user input that would pass local URL validation but be treated by Chrome and Firefox as external URLs is: /\nexample.com.'
